channel.title: Daragh Hollman
channel.description: Recent content in Science on Daragh Hollman

item.title: About
item.description:
About Me

Bio

From Dublin etc…

Give outline of career to date I guess

I’m a recent graduate in Physics with Astronomy and Space Science from University College Dublin. I’m particularly interested in computing, and its applications to the field.

Research Interests

My research interests are… maths and programming in space science.

From September, I am starting a PhD with the Planetary Magnetospheres group at the Dublin Institute for Advanced Studies. I will be studying Mercury’s magnetosphere and its interaction with the solar environment through analysis of large data sets from the MESSENGER mission, and upon its arrival, BepiColombo.

I’m a Government of Ireland Postgraduate Scholar, fully funded by the Irish Research Council. I have recently graduated from UCD with first class honours, BSc. Physics with Astonomy and Space Science.

Hobbies

Outside of my research, I do lots of other hobbies! I love the outdoors.

  • Whitewater and Sea kayaking
  • Hiking

I also have a huge interest in music, and play the piano and electric guitar!

item.title: Contact
item.description:
Contact me!

Please don’t hesitate to contact me with any queries! You can reach me at the following email address:

  • dhollman@cp.dias.ie

You will also find me on the following sites:

  • Github
  • LinkedIn
  • Instagram

I am currently not searching for work, however my CV is available here.

item.title: Horizons Wallpaper
item.description:
Horizons Wallpaper

A python script to plot a live wallpaper of the solar system from the orbital information of specified objects.

This script queries the API of NASA JPL’s Horizons System to obtain orbital positions in cartesian coorinates. These are then drawn to an image (flattened to the ecliptic) and saved as a png or jpg to a path of your chosing.

The idea being: if you ran this script every day, and automatically updated your wallpaper, you would essentially have a live wallpaper of the positions of planets, asteroids, and spacecraft.

Horizons Wallpaper is published on Github.

item.title: JUPT
item.description:
Juno Universal Plotting Tool (JUPT)

Outline

In the summer of 2023, together with the DIAS Planetary Magnetospheres group, I led the development and release of a new plotting tool for data from the Juno spacecraft (currently in orbit around Jupiter).

This Juno data plotting tool (named JUPT - Juno Universal Plotting Tool) is the first public open access tool to make the comparison between data from different instruments - including a magnetometer (MAG), plasma (JADE), energetic particles (JEDI), and radio wave (WAVES) instruments. This flexible tool we developed allows for the creation of publication-ready multi-panel plots through the automatic source of data and acompanying spacecraft ephemeris information.

JUPT is incredibly useful for those studying Jupiter’s space environment as it makes data visualisation quick and easy, so that researchers can spend less time on coding and plotting, and more time on science.

JUPT is published on Github and Zenodo, with documentation published with ReadTheDocs.
